seoTitle: How to Add an Image in Power BI (Beginner Tip)
metaDescription: A beginner-friendly walkthrough for adding images, like logos or context visuals, into your Power BI reports.
intro: Images can make a Power BI report feel more branded and complete, whether it's a company logo or a supporting visual. This beginner-friendly tutorial shows how to upload and adjust an image so it fits naturally into your report.

steps.heading: Upload your image
steps.body: Use Power BI's image insert option to upload the image file you want to add to your report.

steps.heading: Adjust image properties
steps.body: Resize and position the image, and adjust settings like transparency so it fits well alongside your other visuals.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for image use
steps.body: Keep images relevant and appropriately sized so they add value without distracting from the data.

steps.heading: Apply it to real reports
steps.body: Use images for scenarios like adding a company logo to client-facing reports or a contextual photo to a presentation.

faq.question: What image file types can I use in Power BI?
faq.answer: Power BI generally supports common formats like PNG, JPG, and SVG for report images.

faq.question: Can I make an image clickable to link to another page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add actions to an image, such as navigating to a bookmark or another report page.

faq.question: Will adding images slow down my report?
faq.answer: Large image files can add some load time, so it's best to use reasonably sized, optimized images.

faq.question: Can I replace an image later without rebuilding the report?
faq.answer: Yes, you can swap out the image file directly through the same image formatting options.
